message:
fix(ipc): prevent OOM from unbounded IPC line reading; test(policy): add env-assignment command candidate tests

Two changes:

1. ipc.rs — Wrap the IPC socket reader with AsyncReadExt::take() bounded
   to MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE+1 bytes before passing to the line reader. This
   prevents a malicious or buggy client from causing OOM by sending an
   unbounded stream of data without newlines. The +1 allows detecting
   truncation at the limit boundary.

2. policy.rs — Add tests for is_command_candidate() edge cases:
   - Environment variable assignments with trailing commands
     (e.g., "FOO=bar rm -rf /") are correctly detected as commands
   - Bare assignments without commands are not flagged
   - Subshell injection in assignments (e.g., "FOO=$(rm -rf /)")
     is correctly detected
   - Plain text and comments are not flagged

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
